 {"id":"bd-10x1","title":"Property Tests: bubbletea message routing","description":"Property-based tests for bubbletea message handling invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify message routing, batching,
and sequencing invariants.

## Properties to Test

### Message Type System
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() matches original type
- [ ] downcast succeeds for correct type
- [ ] downcast fails for wrong type
- [ ] Clone produces equal message

### Batch Execution
- [ ] All commands in batch execute
- [ ] Batch of n produces n results
- [ ] Empty batch produces empty result
- [ ] Nested batches flatten correctly

### Sequence Execution
- [ ] Commands execute in order
- [ ] Later commands see earlier results
- [ ] Empty sequence produces empty
- [ ] Sequence of 1 equals that command

### Command Composition
- [ ] batch(sequence(a,b), c) executes a,b,c
- [ ] sequence(batch(a,b), c) executes (a||b),c
- [ ] Deeply nested compositions work

### Model Update Loop
- [ ] update() called for each message
- [ ] Cmd results become new messages
- [ ] No messages lost in routing

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn batch_executes_all_commands(
        cmd_count in 1usize..20,
    ) {
        let counter = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
        let cmds: Vec<_> = (0..cmd_count)
            .map(|_| {
                let c = counter.clone();
                Cmd::new(move || { c.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst); Message::none() })
            })
            .collect();
        let batch = Cmd::batch(cmds);
        execute_cmd(batch);
        assert_eq!(counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst), cmd_count);
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Message routing always correct
- Composition laws hold
- No lost or duplicate messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:51.016239622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:24.468522463Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:24.468455989Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10x1","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:03.951653255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-10x1","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:51.050012361Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-11wv","title":"Bubbletea: track async event thread via TaskTracker","description":"# Bubbletea Async Event Thread Tracking

## Problem
In program.rs, the async event loop path (run_async / async_event_loop) spawns
an event polling thread at line ~1109:

    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

This thread uses a CancellationToken to know when to stop, but it is NOT
spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking(). The graceful shutdown sequence is:

1. cancel_token.cancel()          // signals all tracked tasks
2. task_tracker.close()           // prevents new tasks
3. task_tracker.wait() with 5s timeout  // waits for tracked tasks

Since the event thread is NOT tracked, step 3 doesn't wait for it. The thread
may still be blocked in event::poll() or event::read() when the async runtime
shuts down. This creates a race: the event thread may try to send on a dropped
tokio channel, causing a panic or silent failure.

## Why It Matters
- Graceful shutdown is incomplete — the event thread outlives the runtime
- In production SSH servers, accumulated leaked threads = resource exhaustion
- The 100ms poll timeout means the thread *usually* stops quickly, but "usually"
  is not "always" — event::read() can block indefinitely on some platforms

## Solution
Replace std::thread::spawn() with task_tracker.spawn_blocking() which:
- Registers the thread with TaskTracker
- Ensures graceful shutdown waits for it
- Proper cancellation via the existing CancellationToken

The spawn_blocking signature:
    task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

This is a minimal change — the thread body stays identical, only the spawn
mechanism changes.

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (async event loop, line ~1109)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event thread spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking()
- Graceful shutdown waits for event thread
- CancellationToken still used for early exit
- Existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.300659380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:26.486946122Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11wv","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.323443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when subtask is done:

 {"id":"bd-11zs","title":"Property Tests: lipgloss dimension calculations","description":"Property-based tests for lipgloss width/height calculations.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify dimension calculation invariants
across all style combinations.

## Properties to Test

### Width Invariants
- [ ] output_width >= content_width (when width set)
- [ ] output_width == width when width > content_width
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_left + padding_right
- [ ] margin adds exactly margin_left + margin_right

### Height Invariants
- [ ] output_height >= content_lines
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_top + padding_bottom
- [ ] border adds exactly 2 (top + bottom) when present

### Unicode Width
- [ ] Wide chars (CJK) count as 2
- [ ] Combining chars count as 0
- [ ] Emoji width handled correctly
- [ ] ANSI sequences don't affect width

### Composition
- [ ] inherit() preserves dimension calculations
- [ ] copy() produces identical dimensions
- [ ] nested styles compute correctly

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn width_never_less_than_content(
        content in "[\\w\\s]{0,100}",
        width in 0usize..200,
        padding in 0usize..20,
    ) {
        let style = Style::new()
            .width(width)
            .padding_horizontal(padding);
        let output = style.render(&content);
        assert!(visual_width(&output) >= visual_width(&content));
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Dimensions always calculated correctly
- Unicode edge cases handled
- No panics for any style combination","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.723939917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659766118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659630014Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lipgloss","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:57.783744084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.743725346Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1bja","title":"charmed_log: Handle RwLock poisoning gracefully instead of panic","description":"# Bug: RwLock Poisoning Causes Application-Wide Crash

## Problem Statement
The charmed_log crate uses .unwrap() on all RwLock read/write acquisitions.
If any thread panics while holding the lock, the lock becomes "poisoned" and
ALL future lock acquisitions will panic, cascading to crash the entire application.

## Affected Code Locations
```
Line 427: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 468: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 475: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 481: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 488: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 494: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 500: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 506: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 512: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 518: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 528: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 579: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 597: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
```

## Root Cause Analysis
The pattern `self.inner.read().unwrap()` assumes the lock will never be poisoned.
However, if a thread panics during:
- Format string processing (e.g., invalid UTF-8)
- Style rendering (e.g., lipgloss issue)
- Any code path holding the lock

Then ALL subsequent logging calls crash with:
`called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: PoisonError { ... }`

## Solution Design
Replace all .unwrap() with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern:

```rust
// Before (panics on poisoned lock):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();

// After (recovers from poisoning):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
```

This pattern:
1. Recovers the inner data even if lock was poisoned
2. Allows logging to continue functioning
3. Is the recommended pattern for "must not fail" operations like logging

## Alternative Considered: Return Result
Changing the API to return Result<(), LogError> was considered but rejected because:
- Breaking API change
- Logging should be fire-and-forget
- Callers shouldn't need to handle logging errors in their business logic

## Implementation Steps
1. Create helper macro or function to encapsulate the unwrap_or_else pattern
2. Replace all 13 occurrences
3. Add test that verifies logging continues after simulated panic
4. Document the recovery behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn logging_continues_after_panic_in_another_thread() {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    
    // Spawn thread that panics while holding lock
    let logger_clone = logger.clone();
    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        // Acquire lock and panic
        // (requires test-only hook to simulate)
    }).join().ok();
    
    // This should NOT panic - logging should continue
    logger.info("After panic", &[]);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 .unwrap() calls replaced with poison-recovery pattern
- [ ] Test demonstrates continued operation after poisoning
- [ ] No performance regression (unwrap_or_else is zero-cost when lock is healthy)
- [ ] Documentation updated to note recovery behavior","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.303042431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.131050035Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.130977760Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - all RwLock accesses use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","panic-fix","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bja","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.329172028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1dsp","title":"Bubbletea: join sync event loop threads on shutdown","description":"# Bubbletea Sync Thread Joining

## Problem
In program.rs, the synchronous event loop (run_with_writer -> event_loop) spawns
three background threads via std::thread::spawn():

1. **External message forwarder** (line ~628-635): Reads from external_rx channel
   and forwards to the main tx channel. Loop breaks when ext_rx disconnects or
   send fails.

2. **Input parser thread** (line ~639-670): Reads raw bytes from stdin, parses
   terminal escape sequences via InputParser, and sends KeyMsg/MouseMsg to tx.
   Loop breaks when stdin read returns 0 or send fails.

3. **Command execution thread** (line ~878-910): Receives Cmd from a channel,
   executes them (which may spawn sub-threads via rayon or std::thread), and
   sends results back to tx. Loop breaks when cmd_rx disconnects.

None of these threads' JoinHandles are stored or joined. When run_with_writer()
returns, the main thread exits and the OS terminates these threads mid-execution.

## Why It Matters
- If a command execution is writing to a file when the process exits, the write
  may be truncated (data loss).
- In SSH mode (wish crate), the process doesn't exit per-connection — leaked
  threads accumulate across sessions.
- Spawned threads that outlive their channels will log "dropped — receiver
  disconnected" warnings, polluting logs.

## Solution
Store JoinHandles from all three thread::spawn() calls. After the event loop
returns, signal threads to stop (drop senders/receivers), then join with a
reasonable timeout (e.g., 2 seconds). If a thread doesn't stop in time, log a
warning and detach (don't block shutdown indefinitely).

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (sync event loop path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three spawned threads tracked via JoinHandle
- Threads joined during shutdown with timeout
- Existing tests pass (cargo test -p bubbletea)
- No resource leaks on normal shutdown path","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:28.083860624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:20.564786671Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dsp","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:28.107565580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"bd-1dsp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when BOTH subtasks are done:

Plus the testing bead bd-1327 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

-{"id":"bd-3h5r","title":"Glamour: unit tests and benchmark for LRU cache","description":"# Unit Tests and Benchmark for Glamour LRU Cache

## Purpose
Verify that the new LRU cache implementation is correct and performant.
Include benchmarks to demonstrate the O(1) vs O(n) improvement.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_cache_hit_returns_same_value
Verify that repeated lookups for the same key return cached value.

### 2. test_cache_eviction_at_max_size
Verify that cache evicts oldest entry when MAX_CACHE_SIZE (256) is exceeded.

### 3. test_lru_ordering_correct
Verify that least-recently-used entry is evicted, not arbitrary entry.

### 4. test_cache_key_hash_eq
Verify StyleCacheKey implements Hash + Eq correctly for all field combinations.

### 5. test_cache_miss_converts_and_stores
Verify that cache misses trigger style conversion and store result.

## Benchmark Tests

### 6. bench_cache_hit_performance
Use criterion or test::Bencher to measure cache hit latency.
Target: <100ns per hit (O(1) operation).

### 7. bench_cache_promotion_regression
Compare old Vec::remove approach vs new LRU approach:
- Create 256-entry cache
- Access entry at position 0 (worst case for Vec)
- Old: should be ~256 element shifts
- New: should be ~1 pointer update

### 8. bench_heavy_style_mixing
Simulate real workload: 20 distinct styles accessed in round-robin.
Measure total time for 10,000 accesses.

## Key Files
- crates/glamour/src/syntax.rs (add tests to module)
- crates/glamour/benches/ (create benchmark suite if criterion used)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 correctness tests pass
- Benchmark shows >10x improvement for promotion operation
- No regressions in existing glamour tests","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:55.587551638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:53.551745676Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","performance","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2oct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:53.551694070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2ymx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:55.603820169Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ygt5","title":"Property Tests: bubbles component state machines","description":"Property-based tests for bubbles component state invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683687463Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-ykth","title":"Demo Showcase: eliminate RwLock poison risk across all pages","description":"# Demo Showcase RwLock Poison Safety

## Problem
The demo_showcase pages use std::sync::RwLock for interior mutability in the
view() method (which takes &self per the bubbletea Model trait). There are 50+
locations across 4 page files that call .write().unwrap() or .read().unwrap():

- pages/logs.rs: ~26 locations (viewport, formatted_content, needs_reformat, last_dims)
- pages/docs.rs: ~46 locations (viewport, rendered_content, needs_render, last_dims, last_theme)
- pages/files.rs: ~25 locations (preview_viewport, last_width, last_theme)
- pages/dashboard.rs: ~6 locations (card_bounds)

If ANY thread panics while holding a write lock, the RwLock becomes "poisoned".
All subsequent .read().unwrap() and .write().unwrap() calls on that lock will
panic, cascading the failure through the entire application.

## Why It Matters
- In SSH mode (wish crate), each SSH session runs its own App instance, but
  the pages share RwLock-protected state within each instance
- The bubbletea event loop catches panics from update() but NOT from view()
  (view is called in the render path which is synchronous)
- A single out-of-bounds access or failed assertion in any page's update()
  while holding a write lock = permanent crash for that session
- This is especially dangerous because view() is called on every frame (60 FPS)

## Solution
Create a helper module or extension trait that wraps RwLock access with poison
recovery. Two approaches:

**Approach A: Extension trait on RwLock** (Recommended)
    use std::sync::RwLock;

    pub trait RwLockExt<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>;
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>;
    }

    impl<T> RwLockExt<T> for RwLock<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> {
            self.read().unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| {
                tracing::warn!("RwLock poisoned, recovering");
                poisoned.into_inner()
            })
        }
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
            self.write().unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| {
                tracing::warn!("RwLock poisoned, recovering");
                poisoned.into_inner()
            })
        }
    }

**Approach B: parking_lot::RwLock**
Replace std::sync::RwLock with parking_lot::RwLock which never poisons. This
is simpler but adds a dependency. parking_lot is widely used and well-tested.
It also has better performance characteristics (no syscall overhead on
uncontended locks).

Recommendation: Approach B (parking_lot) if adding a dependency is acceptable,
otherwise Approach A. Either way, the change is mechanical: replace all
.read().unwrap() and .write().unwrap() with the poison-safe equivalent.

## Key Files
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/logs.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/docs.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/files.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/dashboard.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero .read().unwrap() or .write().unwrap() on RwLock in non-test demo_showcase code
- Poisoned locks recover gracefully (log warning, continue operating)
- All 838 demo_showcase tests pass
- SSH mode sessions survive panics in individual page operations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:36.814344802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:38.157132572Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykth","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:36.837669140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-ykth","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when ALL subtasks are done:
1. bd-3v3x: Create poison-safe mechanism (MUST be done first)
2. bd-2s30: Migrate logs.rs (depends on bd-3v3x)
3. bd-3mwa: Migrate docs.rs (depends on bd-3v3x)
4. bd-2x95: Migrate files.rs (depends on bd-3v3x)
5. bd-2ey1: Migrate dashboard.rs (depends on bd-3v3x)
Plus the testing bead bd-xnq2 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

Definition of done (high level):
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` launches a coherent app with multiple pages/panels and a beautiful theme.
- Every major crate feature above is exercised in a way that looks like a real-world product.
- Documented run instructions + keybindings; no panics; terminal state restored on exit.

Notes:
- Prefer a single cohesive "product" story (e.g., ops console / project dashboard) over a grab-bag.
- Use a strong theming system so everything feels unified.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.126209979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:05.063848802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:05.063752613Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: demo_showcase (49k+ LOC) is a fully functional flagship demo showcasing all charmed_rust crates - bubbletea (App model, commands, timers, mouse, resize), lipgloss (theming, borders, layout), bubbles (16 components in use), glamour (Docs page), huh (Wizard forms), wish (SSH mode), charmed_log (Logs page), harmonica (Animator). 802 tests pass, cargo run works, terminal properly restored.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","flagship","showcase"]}
